```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The Covid pandemic demonstrated the value of county employees who have developed trusted relationships with the farmworker community.",
    "Farmworkers face a plethora of resource barriers, ranging from cultural to language to different immigration statuses.",
    "These barriers may prevent farmworkers from accessing much-needed government assistance and information.",
    "The Farmworker Resource program can help low-income non-native speakers navigate systems.",
    "Establishing trusted county resource centers for farmworkers can potentially save lives.",
    "AB 941 is based on a successful Ventura County program that has served over 1,400 farmworkers and their families since opening.",
    "The Ventura County program assists farmworkers in navigating government assistance and workplace regulations."
  ]
}
```
